Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE вҖ” most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  вҖў User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  вҖў User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  вҖў Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  вҖў A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order вҖ” prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files вҖ” use the right directory per kind:
     вҖў `references/<topic>.md` вҖ” session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     вҖў `templates/<name>.<ext>` вҖ” starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     вҖў `scripts/<name>.<ext>` вҖ” statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong вҖ” fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply вҖ” the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

Cart system, search/filters, product data model, competitor-inspired UX patterns, SEO, and deployment on Docker Swarm behind Traefik.", "tags": ["nextjs", "ecommerce", "tailwind", "docker", "swarm", "paraguay", "product-catalog", "cart", "search"], "related_skills": [], "content": "---

description: Build and deploy a standalone Next.js e-commerce store for Paraguayan clients. Cart system, search/filters, product data model, competitor-inspired UX patterns, SEO, and deployment on Docker Swarm behind Traefik.
tags: [nextjs, ecommerce, tailwind, docker, swarm, paraguay, product-catalog, cart, search]
triggers:
  - build an e-commerce store
  - online store
  - tienda online
  - product catalog

  - el viajero
  - viajero
  - outdoor store
  - nextjs store
  - standalone store
  - product filters
  - ecommerce deployment

# Standalone E-Commerce Store

Build and deploy a production e-commerce store for Paraguayan clients using Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4 + Docker Swarm.

**Pattern origin:** `Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio` вҖ” outdoor/camping/fishing store.

**References:**\n- `references/competitor-ux-analysis.md` вҖ” what El Mohicano, Camping44, Decathlon PY, and Superspuma do differently\n- `references/multi-tenant-hosting-architecture.md` вҖ” how all client sites are hosted on paragu-ai.com (subdomains per client, Docker Swarm + Traefik, Cloudflare DNS)

**Repo structure:**
```
app/                    Next.js App Router pages
в”ңв”Җв”Җ page.tsx            Homepage (hero carousel, stats, featured, new arrivals, promotions, store locator, newsletter, payment icons)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tienda/page.tsx     Full catalog with search, filters, stock badges, wishlist
в”ңв”Җв”Җ login/page.tsx      localStorage auth
в”ңв”Җв”Җ register/page.tsx   localStorage auth
в”ңв”Җв”Җ faq/page.tsx        Accordion with anchor support (#envios, #cambios)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ contacto/page.tsx   Contact info + form
в”ңв”Җв”Җ blog/page.tsx       Blog index
в”ңв”Җв”Җ blog/[slug]/page.tsx Blog posts
в”ңв”Җв”Җ promociones/page.tsx Promotions display
в”ңв”Җв”Җ nosotros/page.tsx   About with timeline + values
в”ңв”Җв”Җ categoria/*/page.tsx Category landing pages (6)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ api/subscribe/route.ts Newsletter storage
components/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ header.tsx          Mega menu + cart badge + search bar
в”ңв”Җв”Җ footer.tsx          3-column footer
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cart-sidebar.tsx    Slide-out cart
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cart-context.tsx    Cart state (React Context + localStorage)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ search-filters.tsx  Search + category/price/sort/stock filters
в”ңв”Җв”Җ product-modal.tsx   Quick-view modal
в”ңв”Җв”Җ promo-carousel.tsx  Rotating offer bar
в”ңв”Җв”Җ hero-carousel.tsx   Full-width image carousel with nav arrows
в”ңв”Җв”Җ whatsapp-float.tsx  Floating WhatsApp button
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cookie-consent.tsx  GDPR-compliant banner
в”ңв”Җв”Җ category-layout.tsx  Reusable category page component
lib/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cart-context.tsx    Cart state management
в”ңв”Җв”Җ wishlist.ts         Wishlist + recently viewed (localStorage)
content/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ es.json            All content (products, FAQ, testimonials, features, promos, footer)
config/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ site.json          Business config (address, hours, delivery, features)
в””в”Җв”Җ images.json        Image URL mappings
```

## Core Architecture

### Data Model (content/es.json)

All site content lives in ONE JSON file. Key sections:

```json
{
  "siteName": "El Viajero",
  "businessName": "El Viajero",
  "navigation": { "items": [...], "ctaText": "Pedir por WhatsApp", "ctaHref": "..." },
  "home": {
    "seo": { "title": "...", "description": "..." },
    "heroCarousel": { "slides": [{"image": "...", "title": "...", "subtitle": "...", "ctaText": "...", "ctaHref": "..."}] },
    "stats": { "items": [{"value": "7", "label": "CategorГӯas"}] },
    "features": { "items": [{"title": "...", "description": "...", "icon": "shield|package|truck|phone|wallet|message-circle"}] },
    "newArrivals": { "products": [...] },
    "featuredProducts": { "products": [...] },
    "bestSellers": { "products": [...] },
    "productCatalog": {
      "categories": ["Camping", "Pesca", ...],
      "products": [{ "name": "...", "category": "...", "price": "Gs. 450.000", "priceBefore": "Gs. 530.000", "description": "...", "brand": "OutdoorPro", "specs": "4 pers | 3 kg | 210cm", "stock": 8, "weight": "3.0 kg", "imageUrl": "...", "isNew": true, "featured": true }]
    },
    "finalCta": { ... },
    "newsletter": { ... },
    "contact": { ... }
  },
  "about": { ... },
  "faq": { "items": [{"question": "...", "answer": "...", "id": "envios"}] },
  "whatsapp": { "defaultMessage": "...", "serviceMessage": "..." },
  "promociones": { "promotions": [...] },
  "footer": { ... },
  "categoryMenu": {
    "camping": ["Carpas", "Bolsas de Dormir", "Sillas", "Linternas", ...],
    "pesca": ["CaГұas", "SeГұuelos", "Cajas", "Redes", ...]
  },
  "paymentMethods": { "icons": ["visa", "mastercard", "mercadopago", ...], "installments": "3, 6 y 12 cuotas" },
  "storeLocator": { "title": "...", "address": "...", "hours": "...", "googleMapsUrl": "...", "whatsappNumber": "..." },
  "cookieConsent": { "message": "...", "acceptText": "Aceptar" }
}
```

### Product Data Requirements

Each product MUST have these fields for the site to work correctly:

| Field | Type | Required | Purpose |
|-------|------|----------|---------|
| `name` | string | вң… | Display name |
| `category` | string | вң… | Must match a category in `categories` array |
| `price` | string | вң… | Format: `"Gs. 450.000"` |
| `priceBefore` | string | вқҢ | Strikethrough old price + OFERTA badge |
| `description` | string | вң… | 1-2 sentences |
| `brand` | string | вң… | Brand name for filter + display |
| `specs` | string | вң… | Technical specs line: `"4 pers | 3 kg | 210cm"` |
| `stock` | number | вң… | 0 = agotado, 1-3 = Гәltimos, 4-5 = pocas, 6+ = en stock |
| `weight` | string | вң… | Delivery calculation: `"3.0 kg"` |
| `imageUrl` | string | вң… | Path to SVG or image |
| `isNew` | boolean | вқҢ | Shows NUEVO badge |
| `featured` | boolean | вқҢ | Shows in featured section |

### Cart System

React Context + localStorage. Key implementation in `lib/cart-context.tsx`:

```tsx
interface CartItem {
  name: string
  price: string
  priceGs: number  // parsed numeric for total
  quantity: number
  imageUrl?: string
  category?: string
  priceBefore?: string
}
```

Cart checkout generates WhatsApp message with all items listed + total. Payment integration (Pagopar/Bancard) is the next step.

### Search & Filters

The `SearchAndFilters` component (`components/search-filters.tsx`) provides:
- **Text search**: Searches name, brand, description, specs
- **Category filter**: Dropdown of all categories
- **Price range**: Min/max in Gs.
- **Stock status**: Todos / En stock / Pocas unidades (вүӨ5) / Agotado
- **Sort**: Default / Menor precio / Mayor precio / A-Z
- **Results count**: "X de Y productos"

AI-generated content **MUST NOT** list the four fake testimonial names (Carlos Mendoza, Maria Gonzalez, Luis Ramirez, Ana Benitez) вҖ” these were auto-generated by an AI script and are NOT real client testimonials. Remove them or replace with real ones from the questionnaire. 

## Design System

**Colors** from `app/globals.css` (Tailwind 4 with `@theme`):
```css
@theme {
  --color-primary: #1B5E20;
  --color-primary-foreground: #FFFFFF;
  --color-secondary: #37474F;
  --color-secondary-foreground: #FFFFFF;
  --color-accent: #1565C0;
  --color-accent-foreground: #FFFFFF;
  --color-background: #FAFAFA;
  --color-surface: #FFFFFF;
  --color-surface-light: #F0F7F0;
  --color-foreground: #1B2A1B;
  --color-muted-foreground: #4A5A4A;
  --color-border: #E0E0E0;
  --color-success: #2E7D32;
  --color-warning: #EF6C00;
  --color-destructive: #C62828;
}
```

**Typography:** Poppins (headings, weight 700), Inter (body, weight 400).

## Competitor-Inspired UX Patterns

See `references/competitor-ux-analysis.md` for the full comparison. Key differences:

1. **Hero carousel** вҖ” replaces static gradient, 3+ slides with CTA
2. **Mega menu** вҖ” hover subcategories, not flat nav
3. **No WhatsApp on product cards** вҖ” kills conversion
4. **Hide OOS products** вҖ” toggle checkbox, filter by default
5. **Category landing pages** вҖ” /categoria/camping, etc.
6. **Payment icons section** вҖ” visual badges + cuotas messaging
7. **Store locator** вҖ” Google Maps embed + address + hours
8. **Bigger price display** вҖ” text-xl is the most prominent element on a card

## Deployment

```bash
npm run build
docker build -t {client}:prod .
docker service update --force {client}_web
```

### docker-compose.yml template
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: {client}:prod
    ports: ["3000"]
    networks: [agent-net]
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.rule=Host(`{subdomain}.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.{router}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

### DNS
Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard:
| Type | Name | Content | Proxy |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| A | `{client}` | `72.61.44.159` | вң… Proxied |

## SEO Checklist

- [ ] Sitemap includes all pages + blog posts + category pages
- [ ] metadataBase in layout.tsx
- [ ] JSON-LD structured data (Store schema)
- [ ] Security headers (CSP, HSTS, XFO, Permissions-Policy)
- [ ] robots.txt + security.txt
- [ ] OG image (1200x630, branded)
- [ ] Per-page meta titles + descriptions
- [ ] Preconnect to Google Fonts

## Known Pitfalls

1. **No real payment gateway** вҖ” cart only generates WhatsApp message
2. **Fake testimonials** (Carlos Mendoza etc.) вҖ” remove before launch
3. **Newsletter writes to local JSON** вҖ” not production-ready
4. **Auth is localStorage MVP** вҖ” no backend, plaintext passwords
5. **Product images are placeholder SVGs** вҖ” replace with real photos
6. **site.json vs content/es.json can drift** вҖ” keep in sync
7. **No inventory backend** вҖ” stock counts are static in JSON

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "vps-hardening-docker-swarm", "description": "Harden a production VPS running Docker Swarm + Compose hybrid. Security (UFW, swap, daemon.json), deploy new services behind Traefik, automated backups, and Traefik reconnection automation. Use when setting up or hardening a single-node Swarm VPS.", "tags": ["VPS", "Docker", "Swarm", "Traefik", "UFW", "security", "hardening", "backups", "production"], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: vps-hardening-docker-swarm
description: Harden a production VPS running Docker Swarm + Compose hybrid. Security (UFW, swap, daemon.json), deploy new services behind Traefik, automated backups, and Traefik reconnection automation. Use when setting up or hardening a single-node Swarm VPS.
trigger: VPS hardening OR VPS security OR harden server OR VPS setup OR Docker Swarm production OR Traefik setup
version: 1.0.0
author: hermes
license: MIT
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [VPS, Docker, Swarm, Traefik, UFW, security, hardening, backups, production]
    category: devops
    requires_toolsets: [terminal]
---

# VPS Hardening вҖ” Docker Swarm + Compose Hybrid

## When to Use
- Setting up or hardening a single-node Docker Swarm VPS
- Adding new services behind Traefik reverse proxy
- After Docker restart/upgrade (services need reconnection)
- Setting up automated backups for Docker infrastructure

## Phase 1: Security Hardening

### 1a. UFW Firewall
CRITICAL: Allow SSH FIRST before enabling, or you get locked out.

```bash
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 22/tcp comment 'SSH'
ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP'
ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS'
# Docker internal ranges
ufw allow from 172.16.0.0/12 comment 'Docker internal'
ufw allow from 10.0.0.0/8 comment 'Docker overlay'
ufw allow from 192.168.0.0/16 comment 'Docker bridge'
# Swarm ports
ufw allow 2377/tcp comment 'Swarm management'
ufw allow 7946/tcp comment 'Swarm node comm'
ufw allow 7946/udp comment 'Swarm node comm'
ufw allow 4789/udp comment 'Swarm overlay'
echo 'y' | ufw enable
```

This automatically blocks any services on non-standard ports (like Qdrant 6333, LiteLLM 4000) from external access while keeping them reachable via Docker networks.

### 1b. Swap Space
Even with 32GB RAM, add swap as an OOM safety net:
```bash
fallocate -l 4G /swapfile
chmod 600 /swapfile
mkswap /swapfile
swapon /swapfile
echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
sysctl vm.swappiness=10
echo 'vm.swappiness=10' >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf
```

### 1c. Docker daemon.json

**PITFALL: `live-restore: true` is INCOMPATIBLE with Docker Swarm mode.**
Docker will fail to start with: `failed to start cluster component: --live-restore daemon configuration is incompatible with swarm mode`

Correct config for Swarm:
```json
{
  "log-driver": "json-file",
  "log-opts": {
    "max-size": "10m",
    "max-file": "3"
  },
  "default-ulimits": {
    "nofile": {"Name": "nofile", "Hard": 65536, "Soft": 65536}
  },
  "storage-driver": "overlay2",
  "metrics-addr": "127.0.0.1:9323"
}
```

After writing to `/etc/docker/daemon.json`:
```bash
systemctl restart docker
```

**PITFALL: Docker restart on Swarm loses locally-built images.**
Swarm services using local images (built with `docker build`, not pulled from registry) will go to 0/1 replicas with "No such image" error. Must rebuild them after restart:
```bash
cd /path/to/project && docker build -t image-name:latest .
# Swarm will automatically pick up the new image
```

## Phase 2: Traefik + Swarm/Compose Hybrid Networking

### The Core Problem
Traefik runs as a Swarm service on an overlay network (`agent-net`). Compose projects CANNOT attach to Swarm overlay networks (`rpc error: network agent-net not manually attachable`).

### Solution: Bridge Networks + Traefik Reconnection
Each Compose project uses its own bridge network. Traefik connects to each bridge network to discover and route to them.

Compose project pattern:
```yaml
services:
  myapp:
    image: myapp:latest
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=myapp-net"   # CRITICAL - see below
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.rule=Host(`myapp.example.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
      - "traefik.http.services.myapp.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    networks:
      - myapp-net

networks:
  myapp-net:
    name: myapp-net
```

**CRITICAL: `traefik.docker.network` label is REQUIRED** when Traefik has `--providers.docker.network=agent-net` (Swarm default). Without it, Traefik tries to route through agent-net overlay and gets 404/502. The label tells Traefik which bridge network to use to reach the Compose container. This was the root cause of new services returning 404 вҖ” Traefik discovered them but couldn't route to them.

**PITFALL: Labels must be at service level (not under `deploy:`) for Compose projects.** Swarm services use `deploy.labels`, Compose services use service-level `labels`.

**PITFALL: Host port conflicts.** If a Compose service maps `ports: - "3000:3000"` and another service already uses that host port, the container won't start. Remove host port mappings when Traefik handles external access вҖ” services only need to be reachable via their Docker network.

### Traefik Reconnection Script
After Docker restart, Traefik loses bridge network connections. Create `/opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
TRAEFIK_ID=$(docker ps -q -f name=traefik_traefik)
[ -z "$TRAEFIK_ID" ] && echo "Traefik not running!" && exit 1

# Re-apply postgres alias on pg-bridge (lost on container restart)
PG_ID=$(docker ps -q -f name=postgres_postgres)
[ -n "$PG_ID" ] && docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge "$PG_ID" 2>/dev/null || true

NETWORKS=(myapp-net another-net)  # list all bridge networks
for net in "${NETWORKS[@]}"; do
    docker network connect "$net" "$TRAEFIK_ID" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Connected: $net" || echo "Already: $net"
done
```

Automate with cron and systemd:
```bash
# Cron @reboot
@reboot sleep 30 && /opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh >> /opt/backups/traefik-reconnect.log 2>&1

# Docker post-start hook: /etc/systemd/system/docker.service.d/traefik-reconnect.conf
[Service]
ExecStartPost=/bin/bash -c 'sleep 15 && /opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh &'
```

### ComposeвҶ’Swarm PostgreSQL Access
Compose containers can't reach Swarm services directly. Solution: bridge network with alias.

```bash
docker network create pg-bridge
# Connect Swarm postgres with a stable alias
docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge $(docker ps -q -f name=postgres_postgres)
# Connect Compose services
docker network connect pg-bridge myapp
```

In Compose files, add pg-bridge as external network:
```yaml
services:
  myapp:
    networks:
      - myapp-net
      - pg-bridge
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: [REDACTED_DB_URL]  myapp-net:
    name: myapp-net
  pg-bridge:
    external: true
```

**PITFALL: The postgres alias on pg-bridge must be re-applied after Docker restart** (Swarm container gets new ID). Add to reconnect script.

## Phase 3: Automated Backups

Daily backup script (`/opt/backups/backup.sh`):
1. `pg_dumpall` from postgres container вҶ’ gzipped
2. Docker volume backup via alpine tar container
3. Config files (stack yamls, .env, Hermes config)
4. Retention: 7 days

Cron schedule:
```
0 3 * * * /opt/backups/backup.sh          # PG + volumes + configs
0 4 * * * /opt/backups/qdrant-backup.sh   # Qdrant snapshots
0 5 * * 0 docker system prune -f --filter "until=72h"  # Weekly cleanup
```

## Phase 4: Monitoring Stack (Prometheus + Grafana)

Deploy a monitoring stack for host + Docker metrics:

```yaml
# /opt/monitoring/docker-compose.yml
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
      - prometheus_data:/prometheus
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    command:
      - "--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml"

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    volumes:
      - grafana_data:/var/lib/grafana
      - ./grafana/provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning
    ports:
      - "3001:3000"  # host 3001 to avoid conflicts
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD: [REDACTED]  prometheus_data:
  grafana_data:
```

Enable Docker metrics in `/etc/docker/daemon.json`:
```json
{
  "metrics-addr": "0.0.0.0:9323",
  "experimental": true
}
```

Node exporter (Swarm global service):
```bash
docker service create --mode global --name node-exporter \
  --publish mode=host,target=9100 \
  prom/node-exporter:latest
```

Prometheus config (`/opt/monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml`):
```yaml
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "node"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["node-exporter:9100"]
  - job_name: "docker"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["host.docker.internal:9323"]
```

**PITFALL: Grafana datasource config** вҖ” only one datasource can be `isDefault: true`. If you get "Datasource provisioning error", remove `isDefault: true` from your datasource YAML.

**PITFALL: Port conflicts** вҖ” check what's on 3000 first with `ss -tlnp | grep :3000`. Use 3001+ for Grafana if taken.

**PITFALL: Compose `deploy:` block** вҖ” in `docker compose` (v2), the `deploy` block is ignored. Use simple `ports:` and `volumes:` instead.

Traefik route for Grafana (`/opt/traefik/dynamic/grafana.yml`):
```yaml
http:
  routers:
    grafana:
      rule: "Host(`metrics.example.com`)"
      entrypoints:
        - websecure
      service: grafana
      tls:
        certresolver: letsencrypt
  services:
    grafana:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:3001"
```

Recommended additions with Traefik routing:
- **Uptime Kuma** (~100MB) вҖ” status.domain.com вҖ” monitoring + alerts
- **n8n** (~230MB) вҖ” n8n.domain.com вҖ” AI workflow automation
- **Open WebUI** (~400MB) вҖ” chat.domain.com вҖ” LLM chat interface
- **Umami** (~100MB) вҖ” analytics.domain.com вҖ” privacy analytics

All need DNS A records pointing to the VPS IP.

## Pitfalls Summary

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Docker won't start after daemon.json | `live-restore: true` + Swarm | Remove `live-restore` from daemon.json |
| Compose can't use Swarm overlay | Overlay networks not attachable | Use bridge networks + Traefik reconnect |
| Swarm service 0/1 after restart | Locally-built image lost | Rebuild: `docker build -t name:latest .` |
| New Compose service unreachable | Traefik not on its network | `docker network connect net traefik-id` |
| Compose can't reach Swarm DB | Different network namespace | pg-bridge with `--alias postgres` |
| Labels ignored in Compose | Labels under `deploy:` | Move labels to service level |
| Umami/n8n can't auth to postgres | User/DB not created | Create DB+user in postgres BEFORE starting |
| Docker prune removes active images | Swarm images are "unused" during restart | Prune AFTER all services are stable |
| fun4me-style: stale network label | Old network exists with wrong labels | `docker network rm` then recreate |
| Stack deploy ignores build: in compose | Must build image separately first | `docker build ...` THEN `docker stack deploy` |
| Stale Traefik router after stack rename | Old service router lingers | `docker stack rm` old stack fully before redeploying |
| Cf API token has no DNS write | Token scope = zone:read only | Add DNS A records manually in Cloudflare dashboard |
| LE rate limited on new subdomain | DNS didn't exist during first LE attempt | Wait 1 hour, DNS must resolve before Traefik sees it |
| Port 3000 conflict on restart | Multiple services bind same host port | Remove `ports:` from Compose вҖ” let Traefik handle routing |
| 404 with Traefik + Compose | Missing `traefik.docker.network` label | Add label pointing to the service's bridge network |
| pg-bridge alias lost | Swarm PG container restarted (new ID) | Re-run `docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge` |
| UFW locks you out | Enabled before allowing SSH | ALWAYS `ufw allow 22/tcp` first |

## Verification

See also: `references/traefik-swarm-tenant-pattern.md` вҖ” pure Swarm tenant deployment patterns (no Compose bridge hacks), DNS setup, and Swarm-specific deploy pitfalls.

```bash
# System
free -h                    # RAM + swap
ufw status                 # firewall rules
cat /etc/docker/daemon.json # log rotation

# Docker
docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}' | sort
[REDACTED_DOCKER_CMD]          # Swarm services (all should show X/X)
docker stats --no-stream   # resource usage per container
docker system df           # disk usage

# Backups
ls -lh /opt/backups/pg/    # recent dumps
crontab -l                 # backup schedules

# Networking
docker network ls          # all networks
# For each compose service, verify Traefik can see it:
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://service.domain.com
```

function.arguments:
{"action": "write_file", "name": "vps-hardening-docker-swarm", "file_path": "references/nexa-tenant-deployment-pattern.md", "content": "# Nexa-Style Tenant Deployment Pattern (Docker Swarm + Compose Hybrid)

This document captures the deployment pattern used for ParaguAI clients on the Hermes VPS. All clients share a single VPS, Traefik instance, and `agent-net` overlay network.

## Architecture

```
User вҶ’ Cloudflare (DNS, CDN, SSL)
         вҶ“
       VPS (72.61.44.159)  
         вҶ“
       Traefik (port 443) вҶ’ Docker Swarm `agent-net`
         вҶ“
       Multiple services, each with own repo + Docker image
```

## Deployment Flow for a New Tenant

### 1. Build the Standalone Next.js App

Each client gets their own directory (`/tmp/{client}-client/`). Start from the dayah-litworks template:

```bash
rsync -a --exclude='.git' --exclude='node_modules' /tmp/dayah-client/ /tmp/{client}-client/
```

Key dependencies for a Next.js 15.5+ standalone app:
- `next` (latest, 15.5+)
- `react`, `react-dom` (19.x)
- `tailwindcss`, `@tailwindcss/postcss`, `postcss`

**KNOWN BUG: Next.js 15.5.x rejects `import "./globals.css"`** with TypeScript error `Cannot find module or type declarations for side-effect import of './globals.css'`. Fixes:
  - Remove the import and accept no Tailwind CSS (works but unstyled)
  - Use a `next-env.d.ts` with `declare module "*.css" {}`
  - Or upgrade to a Next.js 15.6+ or later version
  - In practice for starter sites with minimal CSS, removing the import is the fastest path

### 2. docker-compose.yml Template

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: {client}:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"              # Swarm ingress mode, no host port
    networks:
      - agent-net
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.rule=Host(`{subdomain}.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{router}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.{router}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

**CRITICAL:** `ports: "3000"` without host port mapping = Swarm ingress mode. The service is reachable via overlay network but NOT via host IP:port. Access is only through Traefik. This prevents port conflicts.

### 3. Build & Deploy

```bash
# Build must happen BEFORE stack deploy (stack deploy ignores build: in compose)
docker build -t {client}:prod .

# Deploy
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {client}

# After code changes:
npm run build
docker build -t {client}:prod .
docker service update --force {client}_web
```

**PITFALL:** `docker stack deploy` in Swarm mode ignores the `build:` directive. You MUST `docker build` separately first.

**PITFALL:** After image rebuild, run `docker service update --force` вҖ” the old replicas may keep serving with the old image. If that still fails, scale down to 0 then back to N:
```bash
docker service scale {service}=0
docker service scale {service}=2
```

### 4. DNS (Manual вҖ” CF API Token is Read-Only)

The Cloudflare API token (`/root/.cloudflare-env`) has zone:read scope only вҖ” it can verify tokens and list zones but cannot add/edit DNS records. Each new tenant needs a manual A record:

| Type | Name | Content | Proxy |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| A | `{subdomain}` | `72.61.44.159` | вң… Proxied |

Common DNS names used:
- `dayah` вҶ’ `dayah.paragu-ai.com`
- `el-viajero` вҶ’ `el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`
- `nexa-paraguay` вҶ’ `nexa-paraguay.paragu-ai.com`
- `fun4me` вҶ’ `fun4me.paragu-ai.com`

**PITFALL: The Traefik `Host()` rule must exactly match the DNS name.** `Host(`viajero.paragu-ai.com`)` вү  `Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)`. If they don't match, Traefik returns 404 even though DNS resolves.

### 5. Let's Encrypt

Traefik auto-issues LE certs via the `letsencryptresolver`. First request to the domain triggers the ACME challenge. If DNS was just added and didn't propagate, the first attempt will fail (NXDOMAIN) and Traefik retries automatically. Can force by restarting Traefik:
```bash
docker service update --force $([REDACTED_DOCKER_CMD] -q -f name=traefik)
```

### 6. File Cleanup Before Git Push

ALWAYS have a .gitignore with at minimum:
```
node_modules/
.next/
*.log
.env
.env.local
.turbo/
```

The `.next/standalone/node_modules/` directory generated by `output: "standalone"` is ~15,000+ files. If committed, the repo becomes unusable.

## Clients Deployed

| Client | Subdomain | Repo | Status |
|--------|-----------|------|--------|
| Dayah LitWorks | dayah.paragu-ai.com | Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks | вң… Live, 11 pages |
| El Viajero | el-viajero.paragu-ai.com | Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio | вң… Live, 12 pages |
| Nexa Paraguay | nexa-paraguay.paragu-ai.com | Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay | вң… Live, 15 pages, 4 locales |
| Fun4Me | fun4me.paragu-ai.com | Ai-Whisperers/fun4me | вң… Deployed, placeholder site |
| Alejandro Villamayor | alejandro-villamayor.paragu-ai.com | Ai-Whisperers/alejandro-villamayor | вң… Deployed, placeholder site |
"}
